title: acetosa alpina subsp. amplexicaulis
intro: Acetosa alpina subsp. amplexicaulis, also known as Wood Sorrel or Alpine Wood Sorrel, is a subspecies of perennial herb belonging to the Oxalis genus. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its astringent and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, acting as an antioxidant, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Acetosa alpina subsp. amplexicaulis can be used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties.
